Among Atlanta’s original farm-to-table restaurants.
reservations
reserve
Welcome to The Farmhouse
You'll feel right at home, because Farmhouse guests are seated in what was the summer dining room when The Inn's main house was the Nygren's personal home.
The intimacy and casual atmosphere of its days as a family dining room can still be felt today, while maintaining the fine-dining standards patrons appreciate.
We use organic ingredients from our own garden as well as from Serenbe Farms.
book your event
download events guide
No items found.
The Farmhouse menu updates frequently to highlight the freshest local seasonal ingredients.
As one of Atlanta's original farm-to-table restaurants, we focus on organic ingredients from the restaurant's own garden, from neighboring Serenbe Farms, and other Chattahoochee Hills and regional producers.

Chef Nicolas Bour
Chef Nicolas Bour returned to The Farmhouse in 2018, having been Head Chef for two years in the early days of the Serenbe community. Dubbed a "versatile chef" by Christiane Lauterbach in Atlanta Magazine, Chef Nic has 25+ years of experience including pioneering Iris restaurant in East Atlanta in the early 2000s, and has been highly recognized over the years.
He was selected to cook for President George Bush, the French Olympic Sailing Team, and as a celebrity chef for the Kentucky Derby. He also managed to serve over 14,000 covers in four days for President Barack Obama's inauguration and competed in season 13 of Food Network's "Beat Bobby Flay," with a unique twist on the fritter.

An Informal Affair
Socialize at The Farmhouse Bar, or relax at the corner booth while you enjoy a drink before dinner. Enjoying your meal in our dinning room or outside on our porch with it's relaxing atmosphere and beautiful views. Then, after dinner cozy up with a book in our library.

meta.meta_description: The Farmhouse at Serenbe, one of Atlanta's original farm-to-table restaurants, serves regional cuisine using locally grown, organic ingredients from the restaurant's own garden as well as from Serenbe Farms and other area producers.
